# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based HR management system built with TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript for type safety
- **Build System**: Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- **Routing**: React Router DOM with protected routes and role-based access control
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state management, React Context for global state
- **UI Library**: shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives, styled with Tailwind CSS
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Backend Integration**: Supabase client for database operations and real-time subscriptions

### Key Architectural Patterns
- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of compound components and render props
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic abstracted into reusable hooks (useAuth, useSort, etc.)
- **Layout Components**: AppLayout provides consistent navigation and structure
- **Feature-Based Organization**: Components organized by feature domains (employee-profile, settings, etc.)
- **Protected Routes**: Authentication and authorization handled at route level

## Database & Backend

### Supabase Integration
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Supabase
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with email/password and invite flows
- **Real-time**: Live updates for collaborative features
- **Edge Functions**: Serverless functions for complex operations (payroll generation, PDF creation)
- **Storage**: File uploads and document management

### Database Schema Highlights
- Employee tables with role-based permissions
- Attendance tracking with check-in/out timestamps
- Payroll calculations with multiple compensation types
- Project management with team assignments
- Leave management with balance tracking
- Evaluation system with customizable parameters

## Key Files & Components

### Core Application Files
- `src/main.tsx`: Application entry point, renders App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control utilities

### Layout & Navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout with sidebar
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with menu items
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Top navigation bar with user menu
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Custom navigation link component

### Key Feature Components
- `src/components/employee-profile/AttendanceTab.tsx`: Attendance records display
- `src/components/SearchableEmployeeSelect.tsx`: Employee selection component
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Application logo component
- `src/components/settings/`: Various settings management components

### Utility Libraries
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/format-date.ts`: Date formatting utilities
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking functions
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification sending utilities
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance calculations

## Business Logic & Data Flow

### Authentication Flow
1. User logs in via Supabase Auth
2. useAuth hook manages session state
3. ProtectedRoute component checks authentication and permissions
4. Role-based access control determines available features

### Data Fetching Pattern
- TanStack Query for caching and synchronization
- Custom hooks for data fetching (useQuery)
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Real-time subscriptions for live data

### Form Handling
- React Hook Form for form state management
- Zod schemas for validation
- Controlled components with proper error handling
- File uploads with progress tracking

## Development & Deployment

### Development Tools
- **Linting**: ESLint with TypeScript rules
- **Testing**: Vitest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E
- **Type Checking**: TypeScript with strict mode
- **Code Quality**: Prettier for formatting

### Build Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite configuration with React plugin
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `eslint.config.js`: ESLint configuration

### Environment Configuration
- Environment variables prefixed with VITE_
- Supabase URL and keys required
- Separate configs for development/production

## Common Patterns & Conventions

### Component Patterns
- Functional components with hooks
- TypeScript interfaces for props
- Default exports for page components
- Named exports for reusable components

### Naming Conventions
- PascalCase for components and types
- camelCase for variables and functions
- kebab-case for file names
- UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants

### Styling Approach
- Tailwind CSS utility classes
- CSS custom properties for theming
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach
- Consistent spacing and color system

### Error Handling
- Try-catch blocks in async operations
- Error boundaries for React components
- User-friendly error messages
- Logging for debugging

## Performance Considerations

- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Image optimization and lazy loading
- Query caching and invalidation
- Virtual scrolling for large lists
- Bundle analysis and optimization

## Security Features

- Role-based access control
- Input validation and sanitization
- Secure authentication flows
- API key management
- Data encryption at rest and in transit
